---
title: "`r params$modelName` — PFM Stringency Model Report"
subtitle: "Price Stringency Estimation, Historical Fit, and Scenario Projections for Bulk & Diffuse Sectors"
author: "Political Feasibility Module"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
params:
  cacheDir: "C:/Users/renatoro/Desktop/Input Data/remind_inputdata/cache/default"
  gdxPath: "c:/Users/renatoro/Desktop/Projects/Elevate/code/fulldata.gdx"
  assetDir: "output/stringency_model"
  modelName: "Baseline + Rule of Law"
  reportName: "default"
  instQualityDriversBulk: !r c("Government Effectiveness (WGI)", "Rule of Law (VDem)")
  controlDriversBulk: !r c("GDP per Capita", "Hydro Nuclear Share")
  regionMappingFEBulk: "regionmappingH12.csv"
  includeLaggedBulk: false
  logisticTimeTrendBulk: false
  logisticTimeTrendDiffuse: ~
  useMundlakBulk: false
  instQualityDriversDiffuse: ~
  controlDriversDiffuse: ~
  regionMappingFEDiffuse: ~
  includeLaggedDiffuse: ~
  useMundlakDiffuse: ~
  actorPowerDriversBulk: ~
  actorPowerIndexBulk: "Actor Power Index"
  actorPowerDriversDiffuse: ~
  actorPowerIndexDiffuse: ~
  adoptionInstQualityDrivers: !r c("Government Effectiveness (WGI)", "Rule of Law (VDem)")
  adoptionControlDrivers: !r c("GDP per Capita", "Hydro Nuclear Share")
  adoptionRegionMappingFE: "regionmappingH12.csv"
  adoptionThreshold: 0.5
  adoptionThresholdBulk: 0.30
  adoptionThresholdDiffuse: 0.17
  snapshotYears: !r c(2030L, 2040L, 2050L, 2070L, 2100L)
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 1
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    number_sections: true
    code_folding: hide
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 6
---

<style>
  body { background-color: #f5f7fa; color: #333; }
  .main-container { max-width: 95% !important; }
  .toc-content { padding-left: 0px !important; }
  h1, h2, h3 { color: #2C3E50; font-weight: 700; }
  .nav-tabs { font-weight: bold; margin-bottom: 20px; border-bottom: 2px solid #2C3E50; }
  .nav-tabs > li.active > a,
  .nav-tabs > li.active > a:focus,
  .nav-tabs > li.active > a:hover {
    background-color: #2C3E50; color: #fff;
    border: 1px solid #2C3E50; border-bottom-color: transparent;
  }
  .tab-content {
    padding: 25px; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-top: none;
    border-radius: 0 0 6px 6px; background-color: #fff;
    box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
  }
  .card { background-color: #fff; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #eef2f5;
          box-shadow: 0 4px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.02); margin-bottom: 25px; }
  .card-header { background-color: #f8fafc; border-bottom: 1px solid #eef2f5;
                 padding: 12px 20px; font-weight: bold; font-size: 1.1em; color: #2C3E50; }
  .card-body { padding: 20px; }
  /* Sector selector bar */
  #sectorBar {
    position: sticky; top: 0; z-index: 9999;
    background: #2C3E50; color: white;
    padding: 8px 24px; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px;
    box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.3);
    margin-bottom: 10px;
  }
  .sector-btn {
    padding: 5px 22px; border-radius: 4px; font-weight: 700;
    cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.9em; transition: all 0.15s;
  }
  .sector-btn.active {
    background: white !important; color: #2C3E50 !important; border: none !important;
  }
  .sector-btn.inactive {
    background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15) !important; color: white !important;
    border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.5) !important;
  }
  .chart-description {
    background: #f8fafc; border-left: 4px solid #2C3E50;
    padding: 10px 16px; margin: 10px 0 18px 0;
    font-size: 0.93em; color: #444; line-height: 1.55;
  }
</style>

<div id="sectorBar">
  <span style="font-weight:700;font-size:0.95em;margin-right:4px;">Sector:</span>
  <button id="btn-bulk" class="sector-btn active" onclick="setSector('Bulk')">Bulk</button>
  <button id="btn-diffuse" class="sector-btn inactive" onclick="setSector('Diffuse')">Diffuse</button>
  <span style="font-size:0.8em;opacity:0.65;margin-left:8px;">Click to show only Bulk or Diffuse results throughout the report</span>
</div>

<script>
(function() {
  window.setSector = function(sector) {
    // Update button styles
    document.getElementById('btn-bulk').className    = 'sector-btn ' + (sector === 'Bulk'    ? 'active' : 'inactive');
    document.getElementById('btn-diffuse').className = 'sector-btn ' + (sector === 'Diffuse' ? 'active' : 'inactive');
    // Show / hide sector content blocks directly via CSS
    document.querySelectorAll('.bulk-only').forEach(function(el) {
      el.style.display = sector === 'Bulk' ? '' : 'none';
    });
    document.querySelectorAll('.diffuse-only').forEach(function(el) {
      el.style.display = sector === 'Diffuse' ? '' : 'none';
    });

    // Toggle tab header visibility for duplicate tabs
    var snapshotTabs = [];
    var rankingTabs = [];
    document.querySelectorAll('.nav-tabs li').forEach(function(li) {
      var a = li.querySelector('a');
      if (a) {
        var text = (a.textContent || '').trim();
        // Remove numbers like "8.3 " or "8.4 " to match the name
        var cleanText = text.replace(/^[0-9.]+\s+/, '');
        if (cleanText === 'Snapshot Maps') {
          snapshotTabs.push(li);
        } else if (cleanText === 'Regional Price Ranking') {
          rankingTabs.push(li);
        }
      }
    });

    var updateTabGroup = function(tabsGroup) {
      if (tabsGroup.length === 2) {
        var firstTab = tabsGroup[0];
        var secondTab = tabsGroup[1];
        if (sector === 'Bulk') {
          firstTab.style.display = '';
          secondTab.style.display = 'none';
          if (secondTab.classList.contains('active')) {
            secondTab.classList.remove('active');
            firstTab.querySelector('a').click();
          }
        } else {
          firstTab.style.display = 'none';
          secondTab.style.display = '';
          if (firstTab.classList.contains('active')) {
            firstTab.classList.remove('active');
            secondTab.querySelector('a').click();
          }
        }
      }
    };

    updateTabGroup(snapshotTabs);
    updateTabGroup(rankingTabs);
  };
  // Run after the full page (including rmarkdown's buildTabsets) has loaded
  window.addEventListener('load', function() { setSector('Bulk'); });
})();
</script>

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center", dev = "svg"
)

library(pfm)
library(magclass)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)

source(file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), "src/r/configHelper.R"))
source(file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), "src/r/theme.R"))
source(file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), "src/r/modelHelpers.R"))
source(file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), "src/r/forestPlot.R"))

if (!is.null(params$cacheDir) && nchar(params$cacheDir) > 0) {
  madrat::setConfig(
    forcecache    = TRUE,
    cachefolder   = params$cacheDir,
    mappingfolder = gsub("cache/default", "mappings", params$cacheDir)
  )
}

.model_store <- file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(),
                           getPfmConfig("modelDir", "cache"))
dir.create(.model_store, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
options(pfm.modelDir = .model_store)

plotOutputDir <- file.path(params$assetDir, "plots")
if (!dir.exists(plotOutputDir)) dir.create(plotOutputDir, recursive = TRUE)

# ── Per-sector config resolution (Diffuse falls back to Bulk if NULL) ──────────
resolve_sector <- function(diffuse_val, bulk_val) {
  if (is.null(diffuse_val) || (is.character(diffuse_val) && length(diffuse_val) == 0))
    bulk_val else diffuse_val
}

resolve_actor_index <- function(val, default = "Actor Power Index") {
  if (is.null(val) || !nzchar(as.character(val))) default else val
}

cfg <- list(
  Bulk = list(
    instQuality       = params$instQualityDriversBulk,
    controls          = params$controlDriversBulk,
    regionFE          = if (is.null(params$regionMappingFEBulk) ||
                            nchar(as.character(params$regionMappingFEBulk)) == 0)
                          NULL else params$regionMappingFEBulk,
    lagged            = isTRUE(params$includeLaggedBulk),
    logisticTimeTrend = isTRUE(params$logisticTimeTrendBulk),
    useMundlak        = isTRUE(params$useMundlakBulk),
    actorPower        = params$actorPowerDriversBulk,
    actorIndex        = resolve_actor_index(params$actorPowerIndexBulk)
  ),
  Diffuse = list(
    instQuality       = resolve_sector(params$instQualityDriversDiffuse, params$instQualityDriversBulk),
    controls          = resolve_sector(params$controlDriversDiffuse,     params$controlDriversBulk),
    regionFE          = {
      raw <- resolve_sector(params$regionMappingFEDiffuse, params$regionMappingFEBulk)
      if (is.null(raw) || nchar(as.character(raw)) == 0) NULL else raw
    },
    lagged            = isTRUE(resolve_sector(params$includeLaggedDiffuse,        params$includeLaggedBulk)),
    logisticTimeTrend = isTRUE(resolve_sector(params$logisticTimeTrendDiffuse,    params$logisticTimeTrendBulk)),
    useMundlak        = isTRUE(resolve_sector(params$useMundlakDiffuse,           params$useMundlakBulk)),
    actorPower        = resolve_sector(params$actorPowerDriversDiffuse, params$actorPowerDriversBulk),
    actorIndex        = resolve_actor_index(resolve_sector(params$actorPowerIndexDiffuse, params$actorPowerIndexBulk))
  )
)

adp_cfg <- list(
  instQuality = params$adoptionInstQualityDrivers,
  controls    = params$adoptionControlDrivers,
  regionFE    = if (is.null(params$adoptionRegionMappingFE) ||
                    nchar(as.character(params$adoptionRegionMappingFE)) == 0)
                  NULL else params$adoptionRegionMappingFE
)

adoption_threshold         <- as.numeric(params$adoptionThreshold)
adoption_threshold_bulk    <- as.numeric(params$adoptionThresholdBulk    %||% params$adoptionThreshold)
adoption_threshold_diffuse <- as.numeric(params$adoptionThresholdDiffuse %||% params$adoptionThreshold)
snapshot_years     <- as.integer(params$snapshotYears)
key_regions        <- c("USA", "CHA", "DEU", "IND", "JPN", "BRA", "RUS", "ZAF")
LOG_TRANSFORM      <- TRUE  # always TRUE — matches estimatePriceStringencyModel default

back_transform <- function(x) exp(x) - 1

# ── Resolve GDX path ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
gdx_path   <- params$gdxPath
if (!is.null(gdx_path) && nchar(gdx_path) > 0 && !is_absolute_path(gdx_path))
  gdx_path <- file.path(rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file(), gdx_path)
has_scenario <- !is.null(gdx_path) && nchar(gdx_path) > 0 && file.exists(gdx_path)

tidy_magpie <- function(mag_obj, period_label = "Historical") {
  if (is.null(mag_obj)) return(NULL)
  df      <- as.data.frame(mag_obj)
  reg_col <- if ("Region" %in% colnames(df)) "Region" else colnames(df)[1]
  yr_col  <- if ("Year"   %in% colnames(df)) "Year"   else colnames(df)[2]
  var_col <- if ("Data1"  %in% colnames(df)) "Data1"  else colnames(df)[3]
  val_col <- if ("Value"  %in% colnames(df)) "Value"  else colnames(df)[4]
  data.frame(
    region   = trimws(as.character(df[[reg_col]])),
    year     = as.integer(gsub("y", "", as.character(df[[yr_col]]))),
    variable = trimws(as.character(df[[var_col]])),
    value    = as.numeric(df[[val_col]]),
    period   = period_label,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}
```

```{r load_data, cache=FALSE, include=FALSE}
panelData <- getPanelDataHistoricalCached(
  aggregate = TRUE, y = 2000:2022,
  outputRegionMappingFile = "regionmapping_54.csv"
)

df_hist  <- tidy_magpie(panelData, "Historical")
future_mag <- NULL; df_scen <- NULL

if (has_scenario) {
  future_mag <- getPanelDataScenarioCached(
    gdxFile = gdx_path, aggregate = TRUE,
    outputRegionMappingFile = "regionmapping_54.csv"
  )
  df_scen <- tidy_magpie(future_mag, "Scenario")
}
df_all <- do.call(rbind, Filter(Negate(is.null), list(df_hist, df_scen)))
```

```{r fit_models, cache=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# ── Stringency models ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
fit_str <- list(
  Bulk = estimatePriceStringencyModel(
    data                      = panelData, sector = "Bulk",
    actorPowerDrivers         = cfg$Bulk$actorPower,
    actorPowerIndex           = cfg$Bulk$actorIndex,
    instQualityDrivers        = cfg$Bulk$instQuality,
    controlDrivers            = cfg$Bulk$controls,
    includeLaggedECP          = cfg$Bulk$lagged,
    regionMappingFixedEffects = if (isTRUE(cfg$Bulk$useMundlak)) NULL else cfg$Bulk$regionFE,
    logTransform              = LOG_TRANSFORM,
    logisticTimeTrend         = isTRUE(cfg$Bulk$logisticTimeTrend),
    useMundlak                = isTRUE(cfg$Bulk$useMundlak)
  ),
  Diffuse = estimatePriceStringencyModel(
    data                      = panelData, sector = "Diffuse",
    actorPowerDrivers         = cfg$Diffuse$actorPower,
    actorPowerIndex           = cfg$Diffuse$actorIndex,
    instQualityDrivers        = cfg$Diffuse$instQuality,
    controlDrivers            = cfg$Diffuse$controls,
    includeLaggedECP          = cfg$Diffuse$lagged,
    regionMappingFixedEffects = if (isTRUE(cfg$Diffuse$useMundlak)) NULL else cfg$Diffuse$regionFE,
    logTransform              = LOG_TRANSFORM,
    logisticTimeTrend         = isTRUE(cfg$Diffuse$logisticTimeTrend),
    useMundlak                = isTRUE(cfg$Diffuse$useMundlak)
  )
)

# ── Adoption models (for projection gating only) ───────────────────────────────
fit_adp <- list(
  Bulk = estimateAdoptionModel(
    data                      = panelData, sector = "Bulk",
    actorPowerDrivers         = NULL,
    actorPowerIndex           = "Actor Power Index",
    instQualityDrivers        = adp_cfg$instQuality,
    controlDrivers            = adp_cfg$controls,
    regionMappingFixedEffects = adp_cfg$regionFE,
    compute                   = c(ame = FALSE, predictedProbs = FALSE)
  ),
  Diffuse = estimateAdoptionModel(
    data                      = panelData, sector = "Diffuse",
    actorPowerDrivers         = NULL,
    actorPowerIndex           = "Actor Power Index",
    instQualityDrivers        = adp_cfg$instQuality,
    controlDrivers            = adp_cfg$controls,
    regionMappingFixedEffects = adp_cfg$regionFE,
    compute                   = c(ame = FALSE, predictedProbs = FALSE)
  )
)

# ── Prepare future panel data ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Driver standardization (ADR 0007) must reuse the mean/sd FROZEN on the
# historical panel; otherwise the scenario design is standardized on its own
# scale and the historical-frozen coefficients no longer apply (biased/exploding
# prices, seam discontinuity). We recompute the frozen scaling from the same
# historical panel the models were fit on and pass it via `driverScaling=`.
future_df <- list(Bulk = NULL, Diffuse = NULL)
if (!is.null(future_mag)) {
  for (sec in c("Bulk", "Diffuse")) {
    ap  <- union(cfg[[sec]]$actorPower, NULL)
    ai  <- union(cfg[[sec]]$actorIndex, "Actor Power Index")
    iq  <- union(cfg[[sec]]$instQuality, adp_cfg$instQuality)
    ctl <- union(cfg[[sec]]$controls, adp_cfg$controls)
    fe  <- if (isTRUE(cfg[[sec]]$useMundlak)) NULL else cfg[[sec]]$regionFE
    histPrep <- preparePanelData(
      data = panelData, sector = sec,
      actorPowerDrivers = ap, actorPowerIndex = ai,
      instQualityDrivers = iq, controlDrivers = ctl,
      regionMappingFixedEffects = fe, useMundlak = isTRUE(cfg[[sec]]$useMundlak), lag = 1
    )
    future_df[[sec]] <- preparePanelData(
      data = future_mag, sector = sec,
      actorPowerDrivers = ap, actorPowerIndex = ai,
      instQualityDrivers = iq, controlDrivers = ctl,
      regionMappingFixedEffects = fe, useMundlak = isTRUE(cfg[[sec]]$useMundlak), lag = 1,
      driverScaling = attr(histPrep, "driverScaling"),
      trendFreezeYear = max(magclass::getYears(panelData, as.integer = TRUE))
    )
  }
}
```

```{r helper_functions, include=FALSE}
# ── Fit statistics ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
compute_str_stats <- function(fit) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  m    <- fit$model
  k    <- length(coef(m))
  nObs <- nobs(m)
  ll   <- as.numeric(logLik(m))
  r2   <- if (!is.null(m$deviance) && !is.null(m$null.deviance))
            round(1 - m$deviance / m$null.deviance, 3) else NA
  rmse_log <- sqrt(mean(residuals(m, type = "response")^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  list(k = k, nObs = nObs, loglik = round(ll, 2),
       aic = round(AIC(m), 1), bic = round(BIC(m), 1),
       r2 = r2, rmse_log = rmse_log,
       converged = isTRUE(m$converged))
}

# ── Semi-elasticity: exp(beta)-1 with delta-method SE ─────────────────────────
compute_semi_elasticity <- function(fit, instQualityDrivers = NULL, controlDrivers = NULL) {
  if (is.null(fit) || is.null(fit$coeftest)) return(NULL)
  ct   <- fit$coeftest
  beta <- ct[, 1]; se_beta <- ct[, 2]
  semi <- exp(beta) - 1
  se_s <- exp(beta) * se_beta
  df   <- data.frame(
    term         = rownames(ct),
    estimate     = beta,
    semi_elas    = semi,
    se           = se_s,
    lower        = semi - 1.96 * se_s,
    upper        = semi + 1.96 * se_s,
    z            = ct[, 3],
    p            = ct[, 4],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  # Classify term group
  df$group <- classifyTermGroups(
    df$term,
    actorPowerDrivers  = NULL,
    actorPowerIndex    = "Actor Power Index",
    instQualityDrivers = instQualityDrivers,
    controlDrivers     = controlDrivers
  )
  df
}

# ── MEM in USD/tCO2: beta * mean(fitted price) ────────────────────────────────
compute_mem <- function(fit) {
  if (is.null(fit) || is.null(fit$coeftest)) return(NULL)
  m             <- fit$model
  mean_log      <- mean(fitted(m), na.rm = TRUE)
  mean_price    <- back_transform(mean_log)
  ct            <- fit$coeftest
  beta          <- ct[, 1]; se_beta <- ct[, 2]
  mem           <- beta * mean_price
  se_mem        <- se_beta * mean_price
  data.frame(
    term       = rownames(ct),
    mem        = mem,
    se         = se_mem,
    lower      = mem - 1.96 * se_mem,
    upper      = mem + 1.96 * se_mem,
    mean_price = mean_price,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

# ── 45° Calibration Scatter ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# ── Shared calibration data extractor ────────────────────────────────────────
get_calib_data <- function(fit, outlier_sd = 2) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  m <- fit$model
  obs <- m$y; hat <- fitted(m)
  if (is.null(obs) || length(obs) == 0) return(NULL)
  obs_usd <- back_transform(obs); hat_usd <- back_transform(hat)

  # Get region/year from fit$data when available (correct labels; handles cached models)
  data_full <- fit$data
  if (!is.null(data_full) && "region" %in% colnames(data_full) && "year" %in% colnames(data_full)) {
    na_idx  <- as.integer(attr(m$model, "na.action"))
    ok_idx  <- if (length(na_idx) > 0) setdiff(seq_len(nrow(data_full)), na_idx) else seq_len(nrow(data_full))
    region_col <- as.character(data_full$region[ok_idx])
    year_col   <- as.integer(data_full$year[ok_idx])
  } else {
    train <- m$model
    region_col <- if ("region" %in% colnames(train)) as.character(train$region)
                  else rep(NA_character_, nrow(train))
    year_col   <- if ("timeTrend" %in% colnames(train))
                    as.integer(train$timeTrend + 1999L)
                  else seq_along(obs)
  }

  df <- data.frame(obs = obs_usd, hat = hat_usd, resid = obs - hat,
                   region = region_col, year = year_col, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  resid_sd <- sd(df$resid, na.rm = TRUE)
  df$outlier <- abs(df$resid) > outlier_sd * resid_sd
  list(df = df, resid_sd = resid_sd, outlier_sd = outlier_sd)
}

make_calibration_scatter <- function(fit, sector_name, outlier_sd = 2) {
  calib <- get_calib_data(fit, outlier_sd)
  if (is.null(calib)) return(NULL)
  df_plot <- calib$df; resid_sd <- calib$resid_sd
  n_out   <- sum(df_plot$outlier, na.rm = TRUE)

  # Axis limits based on NON-OUTLIER range so the main distribution fills the chart
  df_in  <- df_plot[!df_plot$outlier, ]
  ax_max <- max(c(df_in$obs, df_in$hat), na.rm = TRUE) * 1.08

  band_x  <- seq(0, ax_max, length.out = 200)
  band_df <- data.frame(
    x    = band_x,
    y_lo = (band_x + 1) * exp(-outlier_sd * resid_sd) - 1,
    y_hi = (band_x + 1) * exp( outlier_sd * resid_sd) - 1
  )
  col_sec  <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse
  sub_note <- if (n_out > 0)
    sprintf("45° = perfect fit | Shaded = ±%g log-SD | %d outlier(s) excluded from view — see table below", outlier_sd, n_out)
  else
    sprintf("45° = perfect fit | Shaded = ±%g log-SD | No outliers", outlier_sd)

  ggplot(df_in, aes(x = hat, y = obs)) +
    geom_ribbon(data = band_df, aes(x = x, ymin = y_lo, ymax = y_hi),
                inherit.aes = FALSE, fill = col_sec, alpha = 0.12) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_point(aes(text = paste0(
      "Country: ", region, "\n",
      "Year: ", year, "\n",
      "Fitted: $", round(hat, 2), "/tCO₂\n",
      "Observed: $", round(obs, 2), "/tCO₂"
    )), color = col_sec, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.7) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, ax_max)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, ax_max)) +
    coord_equal() +
    labs(title    = sprintf("%s Sector — Calibration: Observed vs Fitted Price", sector_name),
         subtitle = sub_note,
         x = "Fitted price (USD/tCO₂)", y = "Observed price (USD/tCO₂)") +
    theme_report()
}

make_outlier_table <- function(fit, sector_name, outlier_sd = 2) {
  calib <- get_calib_data(fit, outlier_sd)
  if (is.null(calib)) return(invisible(NULL))
  df_out <- calib$df[calib$df$outlier, ]
  if (nrow(df_out) == 0) return(invisible(NULL))
  df_out <- df_out[order(-df_out$obs), ]
  tbl <- data.frame(
    Region              = df_out$region,
    Year                = df_out$year,
    `Observed ($/tCO₂)` = round(df_out$obs, 1),
    `Fitted ($/tCO₂)`   = round(df_out$hat, 1),
    `Log-residual`      = round(df_out$resid, 3),
    check.names = FALSE
  )
  kable(tbl, format = "html",
        caption = sprintf("%s — Excluded Outliers (|log-residual| > %g SD)", sector_name, outlier_sd)) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
}

# ── Fitted vs Observed Density (log space) ────────────────────────────────────
make_density_plot <- function(fit, sector_name) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  m   <- fit$model
  obs <- m$y; hat <- fitted(m)
  if (is.null(obs)) return(NULL)
  # Cap x-axis at 99th percentile to prevent extreme outliers from compressing the main distribution
  x_cap <- quantile(obs, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)
  df_dens <- rbind(
    data.frame(value = obs, source = "Observed",  stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
    data.frame(value = hat, source = "Fitted",    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  )
  col_sec <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse
  ggplot(df_dens, aes(x = value, fill = source, color = source)) +
    geom_density(alpha = 0.35, linewidth = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values  = c("Observed" = col_sec, "Fitted" = col_warn)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Observed" = col_sec, "Fitted" = col_warn)) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(NA_real_, x_cap)) +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("%s Sector — Fitted vs Observed Distribution (log scale)", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Dependent variable: log(1 + ECP). Closer overlap = better calibration. X-axis capped at 99th percentile.",
      x = "log(1 + ECP)", y = "Density", fill = NULL, color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_report()
}

# ── Historical Price Trajectories (observed dots + fitted line) ────────────────
make_hist_trajectory <- function(fit, sector_name, key_regs) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  m <- fit$model
  if (is.null(m)) return(NULL)
  obs <- back_transform(m$y)
  hat <- back_transform(fitted(m))

  # Prefer fit$data (full training set with region/year) over the GLM model frame,
  # which only stores formula variables and never includes the 'region' column.
  data_full <- fit$data
  if (!is.null(data_full) && "region" %in% colnames(data_full) && "year" %in% colnames(data_full)) {
    na_idx   <- as.integer(attr(m$model, "na.action"))
    all_idx  <- seq_len(nrow(data_full))
    ok_idx   <- if (length(na_idx) > 0) setdiff(all_idx, na_idx) else all_idx
    region_col <- as.character(data_full$region[ok_idx])
    year_col   <- as.integer(data_full$year[ok_idx])
  } else {
    # Fallback: try the GLM model frame (region absent when using regionFE)
    train      <- m$model
    region_col <- if ("region" %in% colnames(train)) as.character(train$region)
                  else rep(NA_character_, nrow(train))
    year_col   <- if ("timeTrend" %in% colnames(train))
                    as.integer(train$timeTrend + 1999L)   # timeTrend = year - 1999
                  else seq_len(nrow(train))
  }

  if (all(is.na(region_col))) return(NULL)
  df <- data.frame(region = region_col, year = year_col,
                   observed = obs, fitted = hat, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  highlight <- intersect(key_regs, unique(df$region))
  df_key <- df[df$region %in% highlight, ]
  if (nrow(df_key) == 0) return(NULL)
  df_long <- tidyr::pivot_longer(df_key, cols = c("observed", "fitted"),
                                  names_to = "type", values_to = "price")
  ggplot(df_long, aes(x = year, y = price, color = region, linetype = type)) +
    geom_line(data = df_long[df_long$type == "fitted",  ], linewidth = 1) +
    geom_point(data = df_long[df_long$type == "observed",], size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("observed" = "blank", "fitted" = "solid")) +
    scale_y_continuous() +
    facet_wrap(~region, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("%s Sector — Historical Price Trajectories: Observed vs Fitted", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Points = observed ECP | Lines = model-fitted ECP (USD/tCO₂, back-transformed)",
      x = "Year", y = "ECP (USD/tCO₂)", color = "Region"
    ) +
    guides(linetype = "none") +
    theme_report() +
    theme(legend.position = "none")
}

# ── Residual Diagnostics (2 or 3 panels) ──────────────────────────────────────
make_residual_plots <- function(fit, sector_name, instQualityDrivers = NULL) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  m     <- fit$model
  train <- m$model
  resid <- residuals(m, type = "deviance")
  hat   <- fitted(m)
  col_sec <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse

  df_r <- data.frame(
    resid  = resid,
    fitted = hat,
    year   = if ("timeTrend" %in% colnames(train)) as.integer(train$timeTrend + 1999L) else seq_along(resid),
    api    = if ("Actor.Power.Index" %in% colnames(train)) train$Actor.Power.Index else NA_real_,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  p1 <- ggplot(df_r, aes(x = fitted, y = resid)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_point(color = col_sec, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = col_bad, linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted", x = "Fitted (log scale)", y = "Deviance residual") +
    theme_report()

  p2 <- ggplot(df_r, aes(x = year, y = resid)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_point(color = col_sec, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
    geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = col_bad, linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(title = "Residuals vs Year", x = "Year", y = "Deviance residual") +
    theme_report()

  # 3rd panel: Actor Power Index if in model, else first IQ driver, else omit
  p3 <- if (!all(is.na(df_r$api))) {
    ggplot(df_r, aes(x = api, y = resid)) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
      geom_point(color = col_sec, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
      geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = col_bad, linewidth = 0.8) +
      labs(title = "Residuals vs Actor Power Index", x = "Actor Power Index", y = "Deviance residual") +
      theme_report()
  } else {
    # Fallback: first available IQ driver
    iq_safe <- if (!is.null(instQualityDrivers) && length(instQualityDrivers) > 0)
                 make.names(instQualityDrivers[[1]]) else NULL
    if (!is.null(iq_safe) && iq_safe %in% colnames(train)) {
      df_r$iq_val <- train[[iq_safe]]
      ggplot(df_r, aes(x = iq_val, y = resid)) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
        geom_point(color = col_sec, alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
        geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = col_bad, linewidth = 0.8) +
        labs(title = sprintf("Residuals vs %s", instQualityDrivers[[1]]),
             x = instQualityDrivers[[1]], y = "Deviance residual") +
        theme_report()
    } else NULL
  }

  if (!is.null(p3)) {
    (p1 | p2 | p3) + patchwork::plot_annotation(
      title    = sprintf("%s Sector — Residual Diagnostics", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Loess smoother shown. A flat line at zero indicates well-behaved residuals."
    )
  } else {
    (p1 | p2) + patchwork::plot_annotation(
      title    = sprintf("%s Sector — Residual Diagnostics", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Loess smoother shown. A flat line at zero indicates well-behaved residuals."
    )
  }
}

# ── Partial Regression (Component + Residual) Plots ───────────────────────────
make_partial_regression_plots <- function(fit, sector_name, instQualityDrivers, controlDrivers) {
  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
  m     <- fit$model
  if (is.null(m)) return(NULL)
  train <- if (!is.null(fit$data)) fit$data else m$model
  ct    <- fit$coeftest
  if (is.null(ct) || is.null(train)) return(NULL)
  beta  <- coef(m)
  resid <- residuals(m, type = "response")
  col_sec <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse

  # Theory predictors: Actor Power + IQ (continuous, non-interaction, non-FE)
  theory_vars <- make.names(c("Actor Power Index", instQualityDrivers))
  avail <- intersect(theory_vars, colnames(train))
  if (length(avail) == 0) return(NULL)

  plots <- lapply(avail, function(v) {
    if (!v %in% names(beta)) return(NULL)
    x_vals  <- train[[v]]
    y_vals  <- beta[v] * x_vals + resid    # component + residual
    df_cr   <- data.frame(x = x_vals, y = y_vals, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    # Fitted line: just the component (beta * x)
    x_range <- seq(min(x_vals, na.rm = TRUE), max(x_vals, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = 100)
    df_line  <- data.frame(x = x_range, y = beta[v] * x_range)
    
    lbl <- clean_term_plain(v)
    
    ggplot(df_cr, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
      geom_point(color = col_sec, alpha = 0.4, size = 1.5) +
      geom_line(data = df_line, aes(x = x, y = y), color = col_bad, linewidth = 1) +
      labs(x = lbl, y = "Component + Residual") +
      theme_report() +
      theme(axis.title = element_text(size = 9))
  })
  plots <- Filter(Negate(is.null), plots)
  if (length(plots) == 0) return(NULL)
  wrap_plots(plots, ncol = min(3, length(plots))) +
    patchwork::plot_annotation(
      title    = sprintf("%s Sector — Partial Regression Plots", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Component + residual for each theory predictor. Slope = model coefficient."
    )
}

# ── Adoption-gated price projections ──────────────────────────────────────────
# Helper: align a factor column to model's known levels, mapping unknowns to "Other"
.align_fe <- function(df, col, model_levels) {
  if (is.null(model_levels) || !col %in% colnames(df)) return(df)
  vals <- as.character(df[[col]])
  vals[!vals %in% model_levels] <- "Other"     # map unknowns to reference level
  df[[col]] <- factor(vals, levels = model_levels)
  df
}

get_gated_projections <- function(fit_str, fit_adp, fdf, threshold,
                                  str_regionFE = NULL, adp_regionFE = NULL) {
  if (is.null(fit_str) || is.null(fit_adp) || is.null(fdf) || nrow(fdf) == 0) return(NULL)
  m_str <- fit_str$model; m_adp <- fit_adp$model

  # Use only PREDICTOR variables (exclude response) for complete.cases.
  # all.vars(formula()) includes the LHS response; in future panel data
  # the response (ecp) is NA, which would drop ALL rows if included.
  pred_vars_str <- all.vars(delete.response(terms(m_str)))
  pred_vars_adp <- all.vars(delete.response(terms(m_adp)))
  # Exclude regionFE from complete.cases check — we handle its levels manually below
  needed_str <- setdiff(intersect(pred_vars_str, colnames(fdf)), "regionFE")
  needed_adp <- setdiff(intersect(pred_vars_adp, colnames(fdf)), "regionFE")
  all_needed  <- union(needed_str, needed_adp)
  if (length(all_needed) == 0) return(NULL)
  df_cc <- fdf[complete.cases(fdf[, all_needed, drop = FALSE]), ]
  if (nrow(df_cc) == 0) return(NULL)

  # Dynamic region mapper
  .map_region_fe <- function(df, mapping_file) {
    if (is.null(mapping_file) || nchar(mapping_file) == 0) {
      df$regionFE <- factor(rep("Other", nrow(df)))
      return(df)
    }
    mapping <- madrat::toolGetMapping(mapping_file, type = "regional", where = "mappingfolder")
    regionLookup <- stats::setNames(mapping$RegionCode, mapping$CountryCode)
    regFE <- if (all(df$region %in% mapping$RegionCode)) {
      df$region
    } else {
      regionLookup[df$region]
    }
    regFE <- trimws(as.character(regFE))
    regFE[is.na(regFE) | regFE == "" | regFE == "NA"] <- "Other"
    df$regionFE <- factor(regFE)
    df
  }

  df_cc_str <- .map_region_fe(df_cc, str_regionFE)
  df_cc_adp <- .map_region_fe(df_cc, adp_regionFE)

  # Align regionFE factor levels: unknown levels are mapped to "Other" (reference)
  # so predict() doesn't fail on unseen levels.
  str_fe_levels <- if (!is.null(m_str$xlevels)) m_str$xlevels[["regionFE"]] else NULL
  adp_fe_levels <- if (!is.null(m_adp$model) && "regionFE" %in% colnames(m_adp$model))
                     levels(m_adp$model$regionFE) else str_fe_levels
  
  df_cc_str <- .align_fe(df_cc_str, "regionFE", str_fe_levels)
  df_cc_adp <- .align_fe(df_cc_adp, "regionFE", adp_fe_levels)

  pr_adp   <- tryCatch(plogis(predict(m_adp, newdata = df_cc_adp)), error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(df_cc)))
  pred_log <- tryCatch(predict(m_str, newdata = df_cc_str),          error = function(e) rep(NA_real_, nrow(df_cc)))

  # Projection Guards (ADR 0007), matching pfm::projectSpecScenario: the legacy
  # report's own predict() path must clamp the log(1+ECP) response to the
  # in-sample fitted maximum + extrapLogMargin (2) and cap the back-transformed
  # price at priceCeiling (5000), or an out-of-sample driver/trend excursion
  # explodes through expm1() (e.g. CHA Diffuse → 3e11 USD/tCO2). The time-trend
  # freeze tames this at the source; the guard is the safety net.
  extrapLogMargin <- 2; priceCeiling <- 5000
  insResp <- tryCatch(as.numeric(fitted(m_str)), error = function(e) NULL)
  capVal  <- if (!is.null(insResp) && length(insResp) > 0) max(insResp, na.rm = TRUE) + extrapLogMargin else Inf
  if (is.finite(capVal)) pred_log <- pmin(pmax(pred_log, 0), capVal)
  price_usd <- back_transform(pred_log)
  price_usd <- pmin(price_usd, priceCeiling)

  df_cc$pr_adoption  <- pr_adp
  df_cc$price_hat_log <- pred_log
  df_cc$price_hat_usd <- price_usd
  df_cc$price_gated   <- ifelse(df_cc$pr_adoption >= threshold, df_cc$price_hat_usd, NA_real_)
  df_cc
}

# ── Hurdle projections: E[ECP] = Pr(Adoption) * exp(stringency) - Pr * 1 ─────
get_hurdle_projections <- function(fit_str, fit_adp, fdf, str_regionFE = NULL, adp_regionFE = NULL) {
  proj <- get_gated_projections(fit_str, fit_adp, fdf, threshold = 0,
                                str_regionFE = str_regionFE, adp_regionFE = adp_regionFE)
  if (is.null(proj)) return(NULL)
  proj$expected_ecp <- proj$pr_adoption * proj$price_hat_usd
  proj
}

# ── Price trajectory plot (future projections, adoption-gated) ────────────────
make_price_trajectory_plot <- function(proj, sector_name, key_regs, full_facet = FALSE) {
  if (is.null(proj) || nrow(proj) == 0) return(NULL)
  df <- proj[!is.na(proj$price_gated) & proj$year <= 2100, ]
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
  col_sec <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse
  all_r   <- unique(df$region)
  hi      <- intersect(key_regs, all_r)
  df$grp  <- ifelse(df$region %in% hi, as.character(df$region), "Other")
  df$grp  <- factor(df$grp, levels = c(hi, "Other"))
  my_cols <- setNames(c(scales::hue_pal()(length(hi)), "grey80"), c(hi, "Other"))

  y_max <- max(250, quantile(df$price_gated, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE))

  if (full_facet) {
    ggplot(df, aes(x = year, y = price_gated, group = region)) +
      geom_line(color = col_sec, linewidth = 0.7, alpha = 0.8) +
      scale_y_continuous() +
      coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, y_max)) +
      facet_wrap(~region, ncol = 9) +
      labs(title = sprintf("%s — Adoption-Gated Price Trajectories (All Regions)", sector_name),
           x = "Year", y = "ECP (USD/tCO₂)") +
      theme_report() +
      theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 7), strip.text = element_text(size = 7))
  } else {
    ggplot(df, aes(x = year, y = price_gated, group = region, color = grp,
                   alpha = I(ifelse(grp == "Other", 0.3, 1)),
                   linewidth = I(ifelse(grp == "Other", 0.4, 1.2)))) +
      geom_line() +
      scale_color_manual(values = my_cols) +
      scale_y_continuous() +
      coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, y_max)) +
      labs(title  = sprintf("%s — Adoption-Gated Price Trajectories (Key Regions)", sector_name),
           subtitle = "NA = region has not yet crossed adoption threshold",
           x = "Year", y = "ECP (USD/tCO₂)", color = "Region") +
      theme_report()
  }
}

# ── Price Onset Gantt (year of first non-NA price, coloured by starting level) ─
make_price_gantt <- function(proj, sector_name, end_year = 2100) {
  if (is.null(proj) || nrow(proj) == 0) return(NULL)
  df <- proj[!is.na(proj$price_gated), ]
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
  onset <- df %>%
    group_by(region) %>%
    summarise(onset_year   = min(year),
              onset_price  = price_gated[which.min(year)],
              .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(onset_year, onset_price)
  onset$region <- factor(onset$region, levels = onset$region)

  ggplot(onset, aes(xmin = onset_year, xmax = end_year, y = region, fill = onset_price)) +
    geom_rect(aes(ymin = as.numeric(region) - 0.4, ymax = as.numeric(region) + 0.4),
              color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
    geom_text(aes(x = onset_year, label = sprintf("$%.0f", onset_price)),
              hjust = -0.1, size = 2.5, color = "grey20") +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", name = "Starting\nprice ($/tCO₂)") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2025, end_year, 10)) +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("%s — Price Onset Chart", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Bar starts at first year price crosses adoption threshold. Colour = starting price level.",
      x = "Year", y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_report() +
    theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7))
}

# ── Price snapshot map ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
make_price_map <- function(proj, mapping_54, world, snap_year, sector_name) {
  if (is.null(proj) || is.null(mapping_54) || is.null(world)) return(NULL)
  df_yr <- proj[proj$year == snap_year & !is.na(proj$price_gated),
                c("region", "price_gated")]
  if (nrow(df_yr) == 0) return(NULL)
  map_data <- merge(mapping_54, df_yr, by.x = "RegionCode", by.y = "region", all.x = TRUE)
  world_merged <- merge(world, map_data, by.x = "iso_key", by.y = "CountryCode", all.x = TRUE)
  lim_max <- max(250, quantile(df_yr$price_gated, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
  ggplot(world_merged) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = price_gated), color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
    scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma", na.value = "grey85",
                         limits = c(0, lim_max), oob = scales::squish,
                         name = "USD/tCO₂") +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("%s — Projected ECP in %d (Adoption-Gated)", sector_name, snap_year),
      subtitle = "Grey = no adoption projected by this year"
    ) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(legend.position = "right",
          plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"))
}

# ── Price ranking table at snapshot years ─────────────────────────────────────
make_price_ranking <- function(proj, snap_years, top_n = 10) {
  if (is.null(proj) || nrow(proj) == 0) return(NULL)
  years_avail <- intersect(snap_years, unique(proj$year[!is.na(proj$price_gated)]))
  if (length(years_avail) == 0) return(NULL)
  lapply(years_avail, function(yr) {
    df_yr <- proj[proj$year == yr & !is.na(proj$price_gated), ]
    df_yr <- df_yr[order(-df_yr$price_gated), ]
    df_yr$rank <- seq_len(nrow(df_yr))
    head(df_yr[, c("rank", "region", "price_gated")], top_n)
  })
}

# ── Price distribution violin ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
make_price_distribution <- function(proj, snap_years, sector_name) {
  if (is.null(proj) || nrow(proj) == 0) return(NULL)
  df <- proj[proj$year %in% snap_years & !is.na(proj$price_gated), ]
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
  df$year_f <- factor(df$year)
  col_sec <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse
  ggplot(df, aes(x = year_f, y = price_gated)) +
    geom_violin(fill = col_sec, alpha = 0.4, color = col_sec) +
    geom_boxplot(width = 0.15, outlier.size = 1.5, fill = "white", color = col_sec) +
    scale_y_continuous() +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("%s — Cross-Regional Price Distribution at Snapshot Years", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Violin = density | Box = median & IQR | Only adopted regions shown",
      x = "Year", y = "ECP (USD/tCO₂)"
    ) +
    theme_report()
}

# ── Hurdle: E[ECP] trajectories ───────────────────────────────────────────────
make_hurdle_trajectories <- function(hurdle, sector_name, key_regs) {
  if (is.null(hurdle) || nrow(hurdle) == 0) return(NULL)
  df <- hurdle[hurdle$year <= 2100, ]
  if (nrow(df) == 0) return(NULL)
  col_sec <- if (sector_name == "Bulk") col_bulk else col_diffuse
  all_r   <- unique(df$region)
  hi      <- intersect(key_regs, all_r)
  df$grp <- ifelse(df$region %in% hi, as.character(df$region), "Other")
  df$grp <- factor(df$grp, levels = c(hi, "Other"))
  my_cols <- setNames(c(scales::hue_pal()(length(hi)), "grey80"), c(hi, "Other"))
  
  y_max <- max(250, quantile(df$expected_ecp, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE))

  ggplot(df, aes(x = year, y = expected_ecp, group = region, color = grp,
                     alpha = I(ifelse(grp == "Other", 0.3, 1)),
                     linewidth = I(ifelse(grp == "Other", 0.4, 1.2)))) +
    geom_line() +
    scale_color_manual(values = my_cols) +
    scale_y_continuous() +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0, y_max)) +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("%s — Expected ECP: E[ECP] = Pr(Adoption) × Conditional Price", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Prices fade in gradually as Pr(Adoption) rises — no hard threshold cut",
      x = "Year", y = "Expected ECP (USD/tCO₂)", color = "Region"
    ) +
    theme_report()
}

# ── Hurdle decomposition table ────────────────────────────────────────────────
make_hurdle_decomposition <- function(gated, hurdle, snap_years) {
  if (is.null(gated) || is.null(hurdle) || nrow(gated) == 0) return(NULL)
  years_avail <- intersect(snap_years, unique(hurdle$year))
  if (length(years_avail) == 0) return(NULL)
  rows <- lapply(years_avail, function(yr) {
    h <- hurdle[hurdle$year == yr, c("region", "pr_adoption", "price_hat_usd", "expected_ecp")]
    g <- gated[gated$year  == yr, c("region", "price_gated")]
    m <- merge(h, g, by = "region", all.x = TRUE)
    m$year <- yr; m
  })
  df <- do.call(rbind, rows)
  df[, c("year", "region", "pr_adoption", "conditional_price" = "price_hat_usd",
         "expected_ecp", "gated_price" = "price_gated")]
}

# ── Threshold sensitivity ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
make_threshold_sensitivity <- function(fit_str, fit_adp, fdf, sector_name, key_regs,
                                       thresholds = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7),
                                       str_regionFE = NULL, adp_regionFE = NULL) {
  if (is.null(fit_str) || is.null(fit_adp) || is.null(fdf)) return(NULL)
  df_all <- do.call(rbind, lapply(thresholds, function(thr) {
    p <- get_gated_projections(fit_str, fit_adp, fdf, thr,
                               str_regionFE = str_regionFE, adp_regionFE = adp_regionFE)
    if (is.null(p)) return(NULL)
    p$threshold <- factor(sprintf("Threshold = %.1f", thr))
    p
  }))
  if (is.null(df_all) || nrow(df_all) == 0) return(NULL)
  df_key <- df_all[df_all$region %in% key_regs, ]
  if (nrow(df_key) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  df_key <- df_key[df_key$year <= 2100, ]
  if (nrow(df_key) == 0) return(NULL)

  ggplot(df_key, aes(x = year, y = price_gated, color = threshold, group = threshold)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1, na.rm = TRUE) +
    scale_y_continuous() +
    scale_color_viridis_d(option = "D") +
    facet_wrap(~region, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("%s — Threshold Sensitivity Analysis", sector_name),
      subtitle = "Projected ECP under three adoption thresholds. Key regions only.",
      x = "Year", y = "ECP (USD/tCO₂)", color = "Threshold"
    ) +
    theme_report()
}

# ── Convergence map: difference B - C ─────────────────────────────────────────
make_convergence_map <- function(gated, hurdle, mapping_54, world, snap_year, sector_name) {
  if (is.null(gated) || is.null(hurdle) || is.null(mapping_54) || is.null(world)) return(NULL)
  g <- gated[gated$year == snap_year, c("region", "price_gated")]
  h <- hurdle[hurdle$year == snap_year, c("region", "expected_ecp")]
  df <- merge(g, h, by = "region", all = TRUE)
  price_gated_val <- ifelse(is.na(df$price_gated), 0, df$price_gated)
  df$diff <- price_gated_val - df$expected_ecp
  map_data     <- merge(mapping_54, df, by.x = "RegionCode", by.y = "region", all.x = TRUE)
  world_merged <- merge(world, map_data, by.x = "iso_key", by.y = "CountryCode", all.x = TRUE)
  lim <- max(250, quantile(abs(df$diff), 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
  ggplot(world_merged) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = diff), color = "white", linewidth = 0.1) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#2166ac", mid = "white", high = "#d73027", midpoint = 0,
                         limits = c(-lim, lim), oob = scales::squish, na.value = "grey85",
                         name = "Δ ECP\n(USD/tCO₂)") +
    labs(
      title    = sprintf("%s — Convergence Map: Hard-Gated minus Expected ECP (%d)", sector_name, snap_year),
      subtitle = "Red = hard-gating overestimates | Blue = hard-gating underestimates (e.g. not yet adopted) | Grey = missing data"
    ) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(legend.position = "right",
          plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
          plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"))
}
```

```{r proj_setup, include=FALSE}
# ── Ensure fit$data is available for historical trajectory / calibration plots ─
# When a stringency model is loaded from cache, $data is NULL. Reconstruct it
# by calling preparePanelData with the same config used during estimation.
ensure_str_data <- function(fit, sec, cfg_sec, panel_data) {
  if (is.null(fit) || (!is.null(fit$data) && "region" %in% colnames(fit$data))) return(fit)
  td <- tryCatch(
    pfm::preparePanelData(
      data                      = panel_data, sector = sec,
      actorPowerDrivers         = cfg_sec$actorPower,
      actorPowerIndex           = cfg_sec$actorIndex,
      instQualityDrivers        = cfg_sec$instQuality,
      controlDrivers            = cfg_sec$controls,
      regionMappingFixedEffects = if (isTRUE(cfg_sec$useMundlak)) NULL else cfg_sec$regionFE,
      useMundlak                = isTRUE(cfg_sec$useMundlak),
      lag = 1
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (!is.null(td)) fit$data <- td[td$ecp > 0, , drop = FALSE]
  fit
}

fit_str$Bulk    <- ensure_str_data(fit_str$Bulk,    "Bulk",    cfg$Bulk,    panelData)
fit_str$Diffuse <- ensure_str_data(fit_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse", cfg$Diffuse, panelData)

proj_str <- list(
  Bulk    = get_gated_projections(fit_str$Bulk,    fit_adp$Bulk,    future_df$Bulk,    adoption_threshold_bulk,
                                   str_regionFE = if (isTRUE(cfg$Bulk$useMundlak))    NULL else cfg$Bulk$regionFE,
                                   adp_regionFE = adp_cfg$regionFE),
  Diffuse = get_gated_projections(fit_str$Diffuse, fit_adp$Diffuse, future_df$Diffuse, adoption_threshold_diffuse,
                                   str_regionFE = if (isTRUE(cfg$Diffuse$useMundlak)) NULL else cfg$Diffuse$regionFE,
                                   adp_regionFE = adp_cfg$regionFE)
)
hurdle <- list(
  Bulk    = get_hurdle_projections(fit_str$Bulk,    fit_adp$Bulk,    future_df$Bulk,
                                    str_regionFE = if (isTRUE(cfg$Bulk$useMundlak))    NULL else cfg$Bulk$regionFE,
                                    adp_regionFE = adp_cfg$regionFE),
  Diffuse = get_hurdle_projections(fit_str$Diffuse, fit_adp$Diffuse, future_df$Diffuse,
                                    str_regionFE = if (isTRUE(cfg$Diffuse$useMundlak)) NULL else cfg$Diffuse$regionFE,
                                    adp_regionFE = adp_cfg$regionFE)
)

# Maps setup (same pattern as adoption report)
mapping_54 <- NULL; world <- NULL
if (has_scenario) {
  mapping_54 <- tryCatch(
    madrat::toolGetMapping("regionmapping_54.csv", type = "regional", where = "mappingfolder"),
    error = function(e) NULL
  )
  if (!is.null(mapping_54)) {
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(sf))
    world         <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(scale = "small", returnclass = "sf")
    world$iso_key <- as.character(world$adm0_a3)
  }
}

stats_bulk    <- compute_str_stats(fit_str$Bulk)
stats_diffuse <- compute_str_stats(fit_str$Diffuse)

se_bulk    <- compute_semi_elasticity(fit_str$Bulk,    cfg$Bulk$instQuality,    cfg$Bulk$controls)
se_diffuse <- compute_semi_elasticity(fit_str$Diffuse, cfg$Diffuse$instQuality, cfg$Diffuse$controls)
mem_bulk    <- compute_mem(fit_str$Bulk)
mem_diffuse <- compute_mem(fit_str$Diffuse)
```

---

# Executive Summary

<div class="row">
<div class="col-md-4 bulk-only">
<div class="card">
<div class="card-header" style="border-left:4px solid #2980b9;">Bulk Sector Model</div>
<div class="card-body">
<ul>
  <li><strong>Model name:</strong> `r params$modelName`</li>
  <li><strong>Response:</strong> log(1 + ECP_Bulk) — gaussian GLM (identity link)</li>
  <li><strong>Sample:</strong> Region-years with ECP_Bulk &gt; 0</li>
  <li><strong>IQ drivers:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Bulk$instQuality, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Controls:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Bulk$controls, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>`r if (isTRUE(cfg$Bulk$useMundlak)) "Mundlak correction" else "Region FE"`:</strong> `r if (isTRUE(cfg$Bulk$useMundlak)) "ON (within-region group means)" else if (is.null(cfg$Bulk$regionFE)) "None" else cfg$Bulk$regionFE`</li>
  <li><strong>Lagged ECP:</strong> `r cfg$Bulk$lagged`</li>
</ul>
</div></div></div>
<div class="col-md-4 diffuse-only">
<div class="card">
<div class="card-header" style="border-left:4px solid #27ae60;">Diffuse Sector Model</div>
<div class="card-body">
<ul>
  <li><strong>Model name:</strong> `r params$modelName`</li>
  <li><strong>Response:</strong> log(1 + ECP_Diffuse) — gaussian GLM (identity link)</li>
  <li><strong>Sample:</strong> Region-years with ECP_Diffuse &gt; 0</li>
  <li><strong>IQ drivers:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Diffuse$instQuality, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Controls:</strong> `r paste(cfg$Diffuse$controls, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>`r if (isTRUE(cfg$Diffuse$useMundlak)) "Mundlak correction" else "Region FE"`:</strong> `r if (isTRUE(cfg$Diffuse$useMundlak)) "ON (within-region group means)" else if (is.null(cfg$Diffuse$regionFE)) "None" else cfg$Diffuse$regionFE`</li>
  <li><strong>Lagged ECP:</strong> `r cfg$Diffuse$lagged`</li>
</ul>
</div></div></div>
<div class="col-md-4">
<div class="card">
<div class="card-header">Projection Settings</div>
<div class="card-body">
<ul>
  <li><strong>Adoption threshold:</strong> `r adoption_threshold` — prices shown only above this probability</li>
  <li><strong>Adoption model IQ:</strong> `r paste(adp_cfg$instQuality, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Snapshot years:</strong> `r paste(snapshot_years, collapse = ", ")`</li>
  <li><strong>Scenario data:</strong> `r if (has_scenario) paste0("Loaded from ", basename(params$gdxPath)) else "Not provided — projection sections disabled"`</li>
  <li><strong>Historical range:</strong> 2000–2022</li>
</ul>
</div></div></div></div>

```{r exec-summary-table, echo=FALSE}
mem_mean_bulk    <- if (!is.null(mem_bulk))    round(mem_bulk$mean_price[1], 1)    else NA
mem_mean_diffuse <- if (!is.null(mem_diffuse)) round(mem_diffuse$mean_price[1], 1) else NA

summary_df <- data.frame(
  Sector       = c("Bulk", "Diffuse"),
  `R²`         = c(stats_bulk$r2,      stats_diffuse$r2),
  AIC          = c(stats_bulk$aic,     stats_diffuse$aic),
  BIC          = c(stats_bulk$bic,     stats_diffuse$bic),
  `Log-lik`    = c(stats_bulk$loglik,  stats_diffuse$loglik),
  `N obs`      = c(stats_bulk$nObs,    stats_diffuse$nObs),
  `N pred`     = c(stats_bulk$k,       stats_diffuse$k),
  `Mean fitted price (USD/tCO₂)` = c(mem_mean_bulk, mem_mean_diffuse),
  Converged    = c(stats_bulk$converged, stats_diffuse$converged),
  check.names  = FALSE
)
kable(summary_df, format = "html", caption = "Stringency Model Fit Summary") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(2, bold = TRUE,
    color = ifelse(summary_df$`R²` >= 0.5, col_good,
      ifelse(summary_df$`R²` >= 0.25, col_warn, col_bad)))
```

---

# Data Quality Check

```{r data-qa, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
ecp_bulk_name    <- "Effective Carbon Price|Bulk"
ecp_diffuse_name <- "Effective Carbon Price|Diffuse"
for (var_check in c(ecp_bulk_name, ecp_diffuse_name)) {
  df_v <- df_hist[df_hist$variable == var_check & df_hist$value > 0, ]
  cat(sprintf("\n**%s:** %d region-years with positive price across %d regions.\n\n",
              var_check, nrow(df_v), length(unique(df_v$region))))
}

# Check scenario coverage
if (has_scenario) {
  for (sec in c("Bulk", "Diffuse")) {
    fdf <- future_df[[sec]]
    if (!is.null(fdf)) {
      cat(sprintf("**Scenario (%s):** %d region-year rows, %d regions, years %d–%d.\n\n",
                  sec, nrow(fdf), length(unique(fdf$region)),
                  min(fdf$year), max(fdf$year)))
    }
  }
}
```

```{r data-coverage-table, echo=FALSE}
ecp_vars <- c("Effective Carbon Price|Bulk", "Effective Carbon Price|Diffuse")
df_ecp_hist <- df_hist[df_hist$variable %in% ecp_vars & df_hist$value > 0, ]
cov_tbl <- df_ecp_hist %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  summarise(
    `Regions with price` = n_distinct(region),
    `Obs (region-years)` = n(),
    `Min ECP (USD/tCO₂)` = round(min(value), 1),
    `Median ECP`         = round(median(value), 1),
    `Max ECP`            = round(max(value), 1),
    `Year range`         = paste0(min(year), "–", max(year)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
kable(cov_tbl, format = "html", caption = "Historical ECP Coverage (Positive Prices Only)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

---

# Input Data {.tabset}

## Historical Distributions

```{r hist-dist, echo=FALSE, fig.height=5}
ecp_hist_df <- df_hist[df_hist$variable %in% c("Effective Carbon Price|Bulk",
                                                 "Effective Carbon Price|Diffuse") &
                          df_hist$value > 0, ]
ecp_hist_df$sector <- sub("Effective Carbon Price\\|", "", ecp_hist_df$variable)

ggplot(ecp_hist_df, aes(x = value, fill = sector)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, alpha = 0.7, color = "white", position = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(Bulk = col_bulk, Diffuse = col_diffuse)) +
  scale_x_log10(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  facet_wrap(~sector, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Historical Carbon Prices (Adopters Only, Log Scale)",
       x = "ECP (USD/tCO₂, log scale)", y = "Count") +
  theme_report() + theme(legend.position = "none")
```

## Historical Trends

```{r hist-trends, echo=FALSE, fig.height=6}
ecp_key <- df_hist[df_hist$variable %in% c("Effective Carbon Price|Bulk",
                                             "Effective Carbon Price|Diffuse") &
                     df_hist$region %in% key_regions & df_hist$value > 0, ]
ecp_key$sector <- sub("Effective Carbon Price\\|", "", ecp_key$variable)
if (nrow(ecp_key) > 0) {
  ggplot(ecp_key, aes(x = year, y = value, color = sector)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1) + geom_point(size = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(Bulk = col_bulk, Diffuse = col_diffuse)) +
    scale_y_continuous() +
    facet_wrap(~region, scales = "free_y", ncol = 4) +
    labs(title = "Historical ECP Trends — Key Regions",
         x = "Year", y = "ECP (USD/tCO₂)", color = "Sector") +
    theme_report()
}
```

## Historical Variable Statistics

```{r var-stats, echo=FALSE}
all_str_vars <- unique(c("Effective Carbon Price|Bulk", "Effective Carbon Price|Diffuse",
                          "Actor Power Index|Bulk", "Actor Power Index|Diffuse",
                          cfg$Bulk$instQuality, cfg$Bulk$controls))
df_stats_hist <- df_hist[df_hist$variable %in% all_str_vars, ]
stat_tbl <- df_stats_hist %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  summarise(N = n(), Mean = round(mean(value, na.rm=TRUE),3),
            Median = round(median(value, na.rm=TRUE),3),
            SD = round(sd(value, na.rm=TRUE),3),
            Min = round(min(value, na.rm=TRUE),3),
            Max = round(max(value, na.rm=TRUE),3), .groups="drop")
kable(stat_tbl, format="html",
      caption="Historical Variable Statistics (2000–2022)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width=FALSE)
```

## Scenario Projections

```{r scen-proj-check, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (!has_scenario) cat("> **Scenario GDX not provided.** Re-render with `gdxPath` pointing to `fulldata.gdx`.\n")
```

```{r scen-proj-plot, echo=FALSE, fig.height=5}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(df_scen)) {
  api_scen <- df_scen[df_scen$variable %in% c("Actor Power Index|Bulk", "Actor Power Index|Diffuse") &
                        df_scen$region %in% key_regions, ]
  api_scen$sector <- sub("Actor Power Index\\|", "", api_scen$variable)
  if (nrow(api_scen) > 0) {
    ggplot(api_scen, aes(x = year, y = value, color = region, linetype = sector)) +
      geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
      facet_wrap(~sector, ncol = 2) +
      labs(title = "Scenario Actor Power Index — Key Regions",
           x = "Year", y = "Actor Power Index", color = "Region") +
      theme_report()
  }
}
```

---

# Model Estimation {.tabset}

## Forest Plots

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r forest-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.height=7}
if (!is.null(fit_str$Bulk) && !is.null(fit_str$Bulk$coeftest)) {
  df_coef <- coeftestToDataFrame(fit_str$Bulk$coeftest)
  p <- render_forest_plot(df_coef, title = "Bulk Stringency — Coefficient Forest Plot (log scale)")
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "forest_bulk_stringency.png"), p, width=10, height=7, dpi=300)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "forest_bulk_stringency.svg"), p, width=10, height=7)
  }
}
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each row is one model coefficient on the log(1 + ECP) scale. The point estimate is the dot; the horizontal bar is the 95% confidence interval (robust clustered SE by region). A coefficient of β means a 1-unit increase in the predictor multiplies the expected carbon price by exp(β). Coefficients whose CI does not cross zero are statistically significant. Positive coefficients increase the expected price; negative coefficients decrease it.
</div>

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r forest-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.height=7}
if (!is.null(fit_str$Diffuse) && !is.null(fit_str$Diffuse$coeftest)) {
  df_coef <- coeftestToDataFrame(fit_str$Diffuse$coeftest)
  p <- render_forest_plot(df_coef, title = "Diffuse Stringency — Coefficient Forest Plot (log scale)")
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "forest_diffuse_stringency.png"), p, width=10, height=7, dpi=300)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "forest_diffuse_stringency.svg"), p, width=10, height=7)
  }
}
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each row is one model coefficient on the log(1 + ECP) scale. The point estimate is the dot; the horizontal bar is the 95% confidence interval (robust clustered SE by region). A coefficient of β means a 1-unit increase in the predictor multiplies the expected carbon price by exp(β). Coefficients whose CI does not cross zero are statistically significant.
</div>

</div>

## Coefficient Tables

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r est-bulk, echo=FALSE}
if (!is.null(fit_str$Bulk) && !is.null(fit_str$Bulk$coeftest)) {
  ct <- pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_str$Bulk$coeftest) %>%
    mutate(sig = ifelse(pValue < 0.001, "***", ifelse(pValue < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(pValue < 0.05, "*", ifelse(pValue < 0.1, ".", "")))),
           across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))
  colnames(ct) <- c("Term", "Estimate", "Std. Error", "z value", "Pr(>|z|)", "Sig.")
  kable(ct, format = "html", caption = "Bulk Stringency Model — Coefficients (log scale)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    column_spec(6, bold = TRUE)
}
```

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r est-diffuse, echo=FALSE}
if (!is.null(fit_str$Diffuse) && !is.null(fit_str$Diffuse$coeftest)) {
  ct <- pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_str$Diffuse$coeftest) %>%
    mutate(sig = ifelse(pValue < 0.001, "***", ifelse(pValue < 0.01, "**",
                  ifelse(pValue < 0.05, "*", ifelse(pValue < 0.1, ".", "")))),
           across(where(is.numeric), ~round(.x, 4)))
  colnames(ct) <- c("Term", "Estimate", "Std. Error", "z value", "Pr(>|z|)", "Sig.")
  kable(ct, format = "html", caption = "Diffuse Stringency Model — Coefficients (log scale)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
    column_spec(6, bold = TRUE)
}
```

</div>

```{r make-comparison-table-fn, include=FALSE}
make_comparison_table <- function(se_b, se_d) {
  if (is.null(se_b) || is.null(se_d)) return(NULL)
  shared <- intersect(se_b$term, se_d$term)
  data.frame(
    Term                 = shared,
    `Bulk β`             = round(se_b$estimate[match(shared, se_b$term)], 3),
    `Bulk semi-elas.`    = sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * se_b$semi_elas[match(shared, se_b$term)]),
    `Bulk p`             = round(se_b$p[match(shared, se_b$term)], 3),
    `Diffuse β`          = round(se_d$estimate[match(shared, se_d$term)], 3),
    `Diffuse semi-elas.` = sprintf("%.1f%%", 100 * se_d$semi_elas[match(shared, se_d$term)]),
    `Diffuse p`          = round(se_d$p[match(shared, se_d$term)], 3),
    check.names = FALSE
  )
}
```

---

# Model Formula

The stringency model estimates $\mathbb{E}\!\left[\log(1+\text{ECP}) \mid \text{adopted}\right] = X\beta$ — a **gaussian GLM with identity link** on the $\log(1 + \text{ECP})$ dependent variable (back-transformed as $\text{ECP} = e^{X\beta}-1$). This replaces the former Gamma-log link, which double-exponentiated under projection and drove the price explosion (ADR 0007). Drivers are **standardized**, so each coefficient $\beta$ is the additive effect on $\log(1+\text{ECP})$ per **one-standard-deviation** increase in the predictor; $\beta = 0.3^{***}$ means +1 SD multiplies $(1+\text{ECP})$ by $\exp(0.3) \approx 1.35$ (+35%).

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r model-formula-bulk, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(fit_str$Bulk) && !is.null(fit_str$Bulk$coeftest)) {
  cat("**Bulk Stringency Model Equation:**\n\n")
  cat(formatModelEquationLatex(
    coefficients       = pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_str$Bulk$coeftest),
    actorPowerIndex    = cfg$Bulk$actorIndex,
    actorPowerDrivers  = cfg$Bulk$actorPower,
    instQualityDrivers = cfg$Bulk$instQuality,
    controlDrivers     = cfg$Bulk$controls,
    depVar             = "\\log\\left(\\mathbb{E}[\\text{ECP}_{\\text{Bulk}} \\mid \\text{adopted}]\\right)"
  ), "\n\n")
  cat("Significance codes: $^{***}\\ p < 0.01$, $^{**}\\ p < 0.05$, $^{*}\\ p < 0.1$.\n")
}
```

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r model-formula-diffuse, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (!is.null(fit_str$Diffuse) && !is.null(fit_str$Diffuse$coeftest)) {
  cat("**Diffuse Stringency Model Equation:**\n\n")
  cat(formatModelEquationLatex(
    coefficients       = pfm::coeftestToDataFrame(fit_str$Diffuse$coeftest),
    actorPowerIndex    = cfg$Diffuse$actorIndex,
    actorPowerDrivers  = cfg$Diffuse$actorPower,
    instQualityDrivers = cfg$Diffuse$instQuality,
    controlDrivers     = cfg$Diffuse$controls,
    depVar             = "\\log\\left(\\mathbb{E}[\\text{ECP}_{\\text{Diffuse}} \\mid \\text{adopted}]\\right)"
  ), "\n\n")
  cat("Significance codes: $^{***}\\ p < 0.01$, $^{**}\\ p < 0.05$, $^{*}\\ p < 0.1$.\n")
}
```

</div>

---

## Fit Diagnosis {.tabset}

```{r safe-print-repel-fn, include=FALSE}
safe_print_repel <- function(p, path, w = 9, h = 6) {
  if (is.null(p)) return(invisible(NULL))
  result <- tryCatch(
    { print(p); "ok" },
    error = function(e) {
      p_safe <- p
      p_safe$layers <- p_safe$layers[
        !sapply(p_safe$layers, function(l) inherits(l$geom, "GeomTextRepel"))
      ]
      print(p_safe); "fallback"
    }
  )
  tryCatch(ggsave(path, p, width = w, height = h, dpi = 300),
           error = function(e) message("ggsave skipped: ", conditionMessage(e)))
  invisible(result)
}
```

### 45° Calibration Scatter

<div class="bulk-only">
```{r cal-scatter-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.height=7, dev="png", dpi=150}
p_cs <- make_calibration_scatter(fit_str$Bulk, "Bulk")
if (!is.null(p_cs)) {
  if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
    p_plotly <- plotly::ggplotly(p_cs, tooltip = "text")
    p_plotly <- plotly::layout(p_plotly, margin = list(t = 50, b = 50, l = 50, r = 50))
    p_plotly
  } else {
    print(p_cs)
  }
  tryCatch(ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "calib_scatter_bulk.png"), p_cs, width=9, height=7, dpi=300), error=function(e)NULL)
}
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each dot is one region-year observation with positive ECP_Bulk. Dots on the 45° dashed line = perfect fit; above = model underestimate; below = model overestimate. The shaded band spans ±2 log-SD. Outliers (beyond ±2 SD) are **excluded from the view** to preserve scale for the main distribution — they are listed in the table below with their region, year, and exact prices.
</div>

```{r calib-outlier-tbl-bulk, echo=FALSE}
make_outlier_table(fit_str$Bulk, "Bulk")
```
</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">
```{r cal-scatter-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.height=7, dev="png", dpi=150}
p_cs <- make_calibration_scatter(fit_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse")
if (!is.null(p_cs)) {
  if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
    p_plotly <- plotly::ggplotly(p_cs, tooltip = "text")
    p_plotly <- plotly::layout(p_plotly, margin = list(t = 50, b = 50, l = 50, r = 50))
    p_plotly
  } else {
    print(p_cs)
  }
  tryCatch(ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "calib_scatter_diffuse.png"), p_cs, width=9, height=7, dpi=300), error=function(e)NULL)
}
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Same interpretation as Bulk — each dot is a region-year with positive ECP_Diffuse. Outliers are excluded from the axis range and listed below.
</div>

```{r calib-outlier-tbl-diffuse, echo=FALSE}
make_outlier_table(fit_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse")
```
</div>

### Fitted vs Observed Distribution

<div class="bulk-only">
```{r dens-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.height=4}
p <- make_density_plot(fit_str$Bulk, "Bulk")
if (!is.null(p)) print(p)
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Density curves of the observed (sector colour) and model-fitted (orange) log(1 + ECP) values. Closer overlap indicates better calibration. A narrower fitted distribution suggests under-dispersion; a shifted distribution suggests systematic bias.
</div>
</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">
```{r dens-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.height=4}
p <- make_density_plot(fit_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse")
if (!is.null(p)) print(p)
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Density curves for the Diffuse sector — observed vs model-fitted log(1 + ECP_Diffuse). Close alignment indicates the model captures the observed price distribution well.
</div>
</div>

### Price Trajectories (Historical)

<div class="bulk-only">
```{r hist-traj-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.height=8}
p <- make_hist_trajectory(fit_str$Bulk, "Bulk", key_regions)
if (!is.null(p)) {
  print(p)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_traj_bulk.png"), p, width=12, height=8, dpi=300)
} else {
  cat("> Historical trajectory not available (requires non-cached model run).\n")
}
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Dots = observed ECP; line = model-fitted ECP for key regions. Close alignment indicates a good in-sample fit. Systematic gaps suggest the model is missing a region-specific dynamic. Only regions with at least one year of positive ECP appear.
</div>
</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">
```{r hist-traj-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.height=8}
p <- make_hist_trajectory(fit_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse", key_regions)
if (!is.null(p)) {
  print(p)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hist_traj_diffuse.png"), p, width=12, height=8, dpi=300)
} else {
  cat("> Historical trajectory not available (requires non-cached model run).\n")
}
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Dots = observed ECP_Diffuse; line = model-fitted ECP_Diffuse for key regions. Close alignment = good in-sample fit. Persistent gaps indicate unmodelled region-specific effects in the diffuse sector.
</div>
</div>

### Residual Diagnostics

<div class="bulk-only">
```{r resid-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=5}
p <- make_residual_plots(fit_str$Bulk, "Bulk", cfg$Bulk$instQuality)
if (!is.null(p)) {
  print(p)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "resid_bulk.png"), p, width=14, height=5, dpi=300)
}
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** (1) Residuals vs Fitted — flat loess near zero is ideal; curvature suggests functional misspecification. (2) Residuals vs Year — no trend indicates no unmodelled temporal dynamics. (3) Residuals vs Actor Power — no pattern confirms the linear functional form is adequate. Deviance residuals shown throughout.
</div>
</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">
```{r resid-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=5}
p <- make_residual_plots(fit_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse", cfg$Diffuse$instQuality)
if (!is.null(p)) {
  print(p)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "resid_diffuse.png"), p, width=14, height=5, dpi=300)
}
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Same three-panel diagnostic for the Diffuse sector. A flat loess at zero in all three panels indicates well-behaved residuals. Deviations indicate potential misspecification in that dimension.
</div>
</div>

### Partial Regression Plots

<div class="bulk-only">
```{r partial-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=5}
p <- make_partial_regression_plots(fit_str$Bulk, "Bulk",
                                    cfg$Bulk$instQuality, cfg$Bulk$controls)
if (!is.null(p)) {
  print(p)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "partial_bulk.png"), p, width=12, height=5, dpi=300)
}
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** The marginal contribution of each theory predictor to log(1 + ECP) after removing the linear effects of all other predictors. The slope of the red line equals the model coefficient. A steeper positive slope = stronger association with higher carbon prices, conditional on all other predictors.
</div>
</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">
```{r partial-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=5}
p <- make_partial_regression_plots(fit_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse",
                                    cfg$Diffuse$instQuality, cfg$Diffuse$controls)
if (!is.null(p)) {
  print(p)
  ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "partial_diffuse.png"), p, width=12, height=5, dpi=300)
}
```
<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Partial regression plots for Diffuse sector theory predictors. Slope = model coefficient. Confirms whether the linear specification is appropriate for each predictor.
</div>
</div>

---

# Model Interpretation {.tabset}

## Semi-Elasticity Effects

The **semi-elasticity** of a log-link GLM coefficient β is defined as `exp(β) − 1`. It gives the **percentage change in expected carbon price** for a 1-unit increase in the predictor, holding all else equal. For example, β = 0.30 → semi-elasticity = 35%: a 1-unit increase in that variable is associated with a 35% higher expected ECP. Confidence intervals are computed via the delta method: SE(semi) = exp(β) × SE(β). Significance stars are identical to those from the original coefficient test (testing semi = 0 is equivalent to testing β = 0).

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r se-forest-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(se_bulk)) {
  df_se <- se_bulk %>%
    filter(!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)|^regionFE|^timeTrend", term)) %>%
    mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term)), sig = p < 0.05)
  ggplot(df_se, aes(x = semi_elas * 100, y = term, color = group)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower * 100, xmax = upper * 100), height = 0.3, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = sig), size = 3) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 19, "FALSE" = 1), guide = "none") +
    scale_color_viridis_d(option = "D") +
    labs(title = "Bulk — Semi-Elasticity Forest Plot",
         subtitle = "% change in expected price per 1-unit increase. Filled dot = p < 0.05; hollow = not significant.",
         x = "Semi-elasticity (%)", y = NULL, color = "Term Group") +
    theme_report()
}
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each row is one predictor (intercept, region FE, and time trend excluded). The point is the semi-elasticity (% change in expected ECP per 1-unit increase); the horizontal bar is the delta-method 95% CI. Bars extending right of the dashed zero line indicate the variable is associated with higher carbon prices; bars extending left indicate lower prices. **Filled circles** are statistically significant (p &lt; 0.05); hollow circles are not. The semi-elasticity is computed as exp(β) − 1 from the log-link GLM coefficient β, so it captures the multiplicative (percentage) effect rather than the additive (level) effect.
</div>

```{r se-table-bulk, echo=FALSE}
if (!is.null(se_bulk)) {
  tbl <- se_bulk %>%
    filter(!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)|^regionFE|^timeTrend", term)) %>%
    mutate(across(c(estimate, semi_elas, se, lower, upper), ~round(.x, 4)),
           p = round(p, 3),
           sig = ifelse(p < 0.001, "***", ifelse(p < 0.01, "**",
                   ifelse(p < 0.05, "*", ifelse(p < 0.1, ".", "")))),
           `Semi-elas. (%)` = sprintf("%.1f%%", semi_elas * 100),
           `95% CI`         = sprintf("[%.1f%%, %.1f%%]", lower*100, upper*100)) %>%
    select(term, group, estimate, `Semi-elas. (%)`, `95% CI`, p, sig)
  kable(tbl, format="html", caption="Bulk — Semi-Elasticities (% change in expected ECP per 1-unit increase in predictor)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width=FALSE)
}
```

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r se-forest-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.height=6}
if (!is.null(se_diffuse)) {
  df_se <- se_diffuse %>%
    filter(!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)|^regionFE|^timeTrend", term)) %>%
    mutate(term = factor(term, levels = rev(term)), sig = p < 0.05)
  ggplot(df_se, aes(x = semi_elas * 100, y = term, color = group)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower * 100, xmax = upper * 100), height = 0.3, linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_point(aes(shape = sig), size = 3) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 19, "FALSE" = 1), guide = "none") +
    scale_color_viridis_d(option = "D") +
    labs(title = "Diffuse — Semi-Elasticity Forest Plot",
         subtitle = "% change in expected price per 1-unit increase. Filled dot = p < 0.05; hollow = not significant.",
         x = "Semi-elasticity (%)", y = NULL, color = "Term Group") +
    theme_report()
}
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Semi-elasticities for the Diffuse sector. The interpretation is identical to the Bulk plot: each bar shows the percentage change in expected ECP_Diffuse for a 1-unit increase in the predictor. Filled dots are significant (p &lt; 0.05). Compare with the Bulk plot to see which predictors have similar vs divergent effects across sectors.
</div>

```{r se-table-diffuse, echo=FALSE}
if (!is.null(se_diffuse)) {
  tbl <- se_diffuse %>%
    filter(!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)|^regionFE|^timeTrend", term)) %>%
    mutate(across(c(estimate, semi_elas, se, lower, upper), ~round(.x, 4)),
           p = round(p, 3),
           sig = ifelse(p < 0.001, "***", ifelse(p < 0.01, "**",
                   ifelse(p < 0.05, "*", ifelse(p < 0.1, ".", "")))),
           `Semi-elas. (%)` = sprintf("%.1f%%", semi_elas * 100),
           `95% CI`         = sprintf("[%.1f%%, %.1f%%]", lower*100, upper*100)) %>%
    select(term, group, estimate, `Semi-elas. (%)`, `95% CI`, p, sig)
  kable(tbl, format="html", caption="Diffuse — Semi-Elasticities (% change in expected ECP per 1-unit increase in predictor)") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width=FALSE)
}
```

</div>

## Marginal Effects in USD/tCO₂

The **Marginal Effect at Mean (MEM)** translates the log-scale coefficient into policy-relevant USD/tCO₂ units: MEM = β × E[ECP|x̄], evaluated at the sample mean of fitted prices. An MEM of $5/tCO₂ means that a 1-unit increase in the predictor is associated with $5 higher expected ECP, at the average price level in the sample.

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r mem-bar-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.height=5}
if (!is.null(mem_bulk)) {
  df_mem <- mem_bulk %>%
    filter(!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)|^regionFE|^timeTrend", term)) %>%
    mutate(term = factor(term, levels = term[order(mem)]))
  ggplot(df_mem, aes(x = mem, y = term,
                      fill = ifelse(mem >= 0, col_good, col_bad))) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.3, color = "grey30") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous() +
    labs(title = "Bulk — Marginal Effects at Mean (USD/tCO₂)",
         subtitle = sprintf("Evaluated at sample mean fitted price: $%.1f/tCO₂", mem_bulk$mean_price[1]),
         x = "MEM (USD/tCO₂)", y = NULL) +
    theme_report()
}
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each bar shows the dollar-value impact on expected ECP_Bulk of a 1-unit increase in the predictor, evaluated at the average fitted price in the sample. Green bars increase the expected price; red bars decrease it. The whiskers are 95% CIs (delta method). This chart is complementary to the semi-elasticity plot: semi-elasticities show the *percentage* change and are independent of price level; MEMs show the *absolute* USD impact, which scales with the baseline price level.
</div>

```{r mem-table-bulk, echo=FALSE}
if (!is.null(mem_bulk)) {
  tbl <- mem_bulk %>%
    filter(!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)|^regionFE|^timeTrend", term)) %>%
    mutate(across(c(mem, se, lower, upper), ~round(.x, 2)),
           `MEM (USD/tCO₂)` = sprintf("%.2f", mem),
           `95% CI` = sprintf("[%.2f, %.2f]", lower, upper)) %>%
    select(term, `MEM (USD/tCO₂)`, `95% CI`)
  kable(tbl, format="html",
```
