        caption=sprintf("Bulk — Marginal Effects at Mean (USD/tCO₂), evaluated at $%.1f/tCO₂",
                        mem_bulk$mean_price[1])) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width=FALSE)
}
```

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r mem-bar-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.height=5}
if (!is.null(mem_diffuse)) {
  df_mem <- mem_diffuse %>%
    filter(!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)|^regionFE|^timeTrend", term)) %>%
    mutate(term = factor(term, levels = term[order(mem)]))
  ggplot(df_mem, aes(x = mem, y = term,
                      fill = ifelse(mem >= 0, col_good, col_bad))) +
    geom_col(alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper), height = 0.3, color = "grey30") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    scale_fill_identity() +
    scale_x_continuous() +
    labs(title = "Diffuse — Marginal Effects at Mean (USD/tCO₂)",
         subtitle = sprintf("Evaluated at sample mean fitted price: $%.1f/tCO₂", mem_diffuse$mean_price[1]),
         x = "MEM (USD/tCO₂)", y = NULL) +
    theme_report()
}
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** MEM for the Diffuse sector. Each bar shows the USD/tCO₂ impact of a 1-unit increase in the predictor at the average Diffuse ECP level. Comparing this chart to the Bulk MEM chart reveals whether the same predictor has a larger or smaller absolute dollar impact across sectors (driven by differences in both the coefficient magnitude and the baseline price level).
</div>

```{r mem-table-diffuse, echo=FALSE}
if (!is.null(mem_diffuse)) {
  tbl <- mem_diffuse %>%
    filter(!grepl("^\\(Intercept\\)|^regionFE|^timeTrend", term)) %>%
    mutate(across(c(mem, se, lower, upper), ~round(.x, 2)),
           `MEM (USD/tCO₂)` = sprintf("%.2f", mem),
           `95% CI` = sprintf("[%.2f, %.2f]", lower, upper)) %>%
    select(term, `MEM (USD/tCO₂)`, `95% CI`)
  kable(tbl, format="html",
        caption=sprintf("Diffuse — Marginal Effects at Mean (USD/tCO₂), evaluated at $%.1f/tCO₂",
                        mem_diffuse$mean_price[1])) %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width=FALSE)
}
```

</div>

---

# Future Projections (Adoption-Gated) {.tabset}

```{r proj-gate-check, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (!has_scenario) {
  cat("> [!WARNING]\n> **Scenario GDX not provided.** All projection sections below are disabled.\n\n")
}
```

## Price Trajectories

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r price-traj-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Bulk)) {
  p <- make_price_trajectory_plot(proj_str$Bulk, "Bulk", key_regions)
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "price_traj_bulk.png"), p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "price_traj_bulk.svg"), p, width=12, height=6)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each line shows the projected ECP_Bulk trajectory for one region, **conditional on adoption** (a price line only appears once the region's Pr(Adoption) ≥ `r adoption_threshold`). Key regions are highlighted in distinct colours; all other regions are shown in grey. A line that starts late (or is absent) means that region is not projected to cross the adoption threshold until later in the scenario. Lines that appear mid-way through reflect "late adopters". The y-axis is in USD/tCO₂ (back-transformed from the log-scale model). The full-facet view below shows all regions individually.
</div>

```{r price-traj-bulk-full, echo=FALSE, fig.width=16, fig.height=20, class.output="fold-hide"}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Bulk)) {
  p <- make_price_trajectory_plot(proj_str$Bulk, "Bulk", key_regions, full_facet = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(p)) print(p)
}
```

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r price-traj-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Diffuse)) {
  p <- make_price_trajectory_plot(proj_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse", key_regions)
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "price_traj_diffuse.png"), p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "price_traj_diffuse.svg"), p, width=12, height=6)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Projected ECP_Diffuse conditional on adoption. Lines only appear once Pr(Diffuse Adoption) ≥ threshold. Regions absent from the chart are not projected to cross the adoption threshold in the scenario window.
</div>

```{r price-traj-diffuse-full, echo=FALSE, fig.width=16, fig.height=20, class.output="fold-hide"}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Diffuse)) {
  p <- make_price_trajectory_plot(proj_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse", key_regions, full_facet = TRUE)
  if (!is.null(p)) print(p)
}
```

</div>

## Price Onset Chart

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r gantt-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=16}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Bulk)) {
  p <- make_price_gantt(proj_str$Bulk, "Bulk")
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "gantt_bulk.png"), p, width=12, height=16, dpi=300)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each row is one region; the bar starts at the first year the region crosses the adoption threshold. Bar colour = starting price level (warmer = higher). Regions sorted from earliest to latest adopter. Provides a "when and at what level" summary of the Bulk pricing rollout.
</div>

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r gantt-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=16}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Diffuse)) {
  p <- make_price_gantt(proj_str$Diffuse, "Diffuse")
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "gantt_diffuse.png"), p, width=12, height=16, dpi=300)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Price onset chart for the Diffuse sector. Compare with Bulk to see whether sectors tend to be adopted at similar times or one leads the other.
</div>

</div>

```{r maps-main, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Bulk) && !is.null(mapping_54) && !is.null(world)) {
  # Bulk snapshot maps
  cat('<div class="bulk-only">\n\n')
  cat('## Snapshot Maps {.tabset}\n\n')
  for (yr in snapshot_years) {
    cat(sprintf("\n### %d\n\n", yr))
    p <- make_price_map(proj_str$Bulk, mapping_54, world, yr, "Bulk")
    if (!is.null(p)) {
      print(p)
      ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, sprintf("map_price_bulk_%d.png", yr)), p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300)
    }
    cat("\n\n")
  }
  cat('\n</div>\n\n')

  # Diffuse snapshot maps
  cat('<div class="diffuse-only">\n\n')
  cat('## Snapshot Maps {.tabset}\n\n')
  for (yr in snapshot_years) {
    cat(sprintf("\n### %d\n\n", yr))
    p <- make_price_map(proj_str$Diffuse, mapping_54, world, yr, "Diffuse")
    if (!is.null(p)) {
      print(p)
      ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, sprintf("map_price_diffuse_%d.png", yr)), p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300)
    }
    cat("\n\n")
  }
  cat('\n</div>\n\n')
} else {
  cat('\n## Snapshot Maps\n\n> Map rendering requires scenario data and regionmapping_54.csv.\n\n')
}
```

```{r ranking-main, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
# Bulk Regional Price Ranking
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Bulk)) {
  cat('<div class="bulk-only">\n\n')
  cat('## Regional Price Ranking {.tabset}\n\n')
  ranks_bulk <- make_price_ranking(proj_str$Bulk, snapshot_years, top_n = 15)
  if (!is.null(ranks_bulk)) {
    for (i in seq_along(ranks_bulk)) {
      yr  <- snapshot_years[i]
      tbl <- ranks_bulk[[i]]
      names(tbl) <- c("Rank", "Region", "ECP (USD/tCO₂)")
      tbl[, 3] <- round(tbl[, 3], 1)
      cat(sprintf("\n### %d\n\n", yr))
      cat(sprintf("\n**Top 15 regions by projected ECP in %d:**\n\n", yr))
      print(kable(tbl, format="html") %>%
        kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                      full_width=FALSE))
      cat("\n\n")
    }
  }
  cat('\n</div>\n\n')
}

# Diffuse Regional Price Ranking
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Diffuse)) {
  cat('<div class="diffuse-only">\n\n')
  cat('## Regional Price Ranking {.tabset}\n\n')
  ranks_diff <- make_price_ranking(proj_str$Diffuse, snapshot_years, top_n = 15)
  if (!is.null(ranks_diff)) {
    for (i in seq_along(ranks_diff)) {
      yr  <- snapshot_years[i]
      tbl <- ranks_diff[[i]]
      names(tbl) <- c("Rank", "Region", "ECP (USD/tCO₂)")
      tbl[, 3] <- round(tbl[, 3], 1)
      cat(sprintf("\n### %d\n\n", yr))
      cat(sprintf("\n**Top 15 regions by projected ECP in %d:**\n\n", yr))
      print(kable(tbl, format="html") %>%
        kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                      full_width=FALSE))
      cat("\n\n")
    }
  }
  cat('\n</div>\n\n')
}

if (!has_scenario) {
  cat('\n## Regional Price Ranking\n\n> Scenario data not available.\n\n')
}
```

## Price Distribution Over Time

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r dist-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Bulk)) {
  p <- make_price_distribution(proj_str$Bulk, snapshot_years, "Bulk")
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "dist_bulk.png"), p, width=10, height=6, dpi=300)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Violin + box plots of projected ECP_Bulk across adopted regions at snapshot years. The violin = full distribution; the box = median & IQR. Only adopted regions shown. A widening violin = more price heterogeneity over time; a rising median = overall upward price trend.
</div>

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r dist-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(proj_str$Diffuse)) {
  p <- make_price_distribution(proj_str$Diffuse, snapshot_years, "Diffuse")
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "dist_diffuse.png"), p, width=10, height=6, dpi=300)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Distribution of projected ECP_Diffuse at snapshot years (adopted regions only). Compare with the Bulk distribution to assess whether diffuse sector prices are higher or lower on average, and whether they are more or less dispersed.
</div>

</div>

---

# Expected Carbon Price — Full Hurdle Model {.tabset}

This section shows the full two-stage hurdle estimate: **E[ECP] = Pr(Adoption) × Conditional Price**. Unlike Section 6 which hard-gates at the adoption threshold, here the price fades in continuously as adoption probability rises, giving the probabilistically correct expected carbon price.

```{r hurdle-check, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
if (!has_scenario) cat("> **Scenario GDX not provided.** This section requires scenario data.\n\n")
```

## Expected Price Trajectories

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r hurdle-traj-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(hurdle$Bulk)) {
  p <- make_hurdle_trajectories(hurdle$Bulk, "Bulk", key_regions)
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hurdle_traj_bulk.png"), p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hurdle_traj_bulk.svg"), p, width=12, height=6)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** E[ECP_Bulk] = Pr(Adoption) × Conditional_Price. The price fades in gradually as adoption probability rises — no hard cliff. A region with Pr(Adoption) = 0.4 and conditional price $20/tCO₂ shows expected ECP = $8/tCO₂.
</div>

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r hurdle-traj-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=6}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(hurdle$Diffuse)) {
  p <- make_hurdle_trajectories(hurdle$Diffuse, "Diffuse", key_regions)
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hurdle_traj_diffuse.png"), p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "hurdle_traj_diffuse.svg"), p, width=12, height=6)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** E[ECP_Diffuse] = Pr(Diffuse Adoption) × Conditional Diffuse Price. Same gradual fade-in interpretation as Bulk.
</div>

</div>

```{r hurdle-decomp, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
has_bulk_decomp    <- has_scenario && !is.null(hurdle$Bulk)   && !is.null(proj_str$Bulk)
has_diffuse_decomp <- has_scenario && !is.null(hurdle$Diffuse) && !is.null(proj_str$Diffuse)

if (has_bulk_decomp || has_diffuse_decomp) {
  cat('## Hurdle Decomposition {.tabset}\n\n')
  cat('Shows Pr(Adoption), Conditional Price, Expected ECP, and the hard-gated price side-by-side. Where hard-gated and expected diverge most indicates where the threshold assumption is load-bearing.\n\n')
  for (yr in snapshot_years) {
    cat(sprintf("\n### %d\n\n", yr))
    if (has_bulk_decomp) {
      h <- hurdle$Bulk[hurdle$Bulk$year == yr, ]
      g <- proj_str$Bulk[proj_str$Bulk$year == yr, c("region", "price_gated")]
      if (nrow(h) > 0) {
        df_dec <- merge(h[, c("region", "pr_adoption", "price_hat_usd", "expected_ecp")],
                        g, by = "region", all.x = TRUE)
        df_dec <- df_dec[order(-df_dec$expected_ecp), ]
        df_dec[, 2:5] <- round(df_dec[, 2:5], 2)
        names(df_dec) <- c("Region", "Pr(Adoption)", "Cond. Price (USD/tCO₂)", "Expected ECP (USD/tCO₂)", "Gated Price (USD/tCO₂)")
        cat('<div class="bulk-only">\n\n')
        cat(sprintf("**Bulk Hurdle Decomposition — %d:**\n\n", yr))
        print(kable(df_dec, format="html") %>%
          kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width=FALSE))
        cat("\n\n</div>\n\n")
      }
    }
    if (has_diffuse_decomp) {
      h <- hurdle$Diffuse[hurdle$Diffuse$year == yr, ]
      g <- proj_str$Diffuse[proj_str$Diffuse$year == yr, c("region", "price_gated")]
      if (nrow(h) > 0) {
        df_dec <- merge(h[, c("region", "pr_adoption", "price_hat_usd", "expected_ecp")],
                        g, by = "region", all.x = TRUE)
        df_dec <- df_dec[order(-df_dec$expected_ecp), ]
        df_dec[, 2:5] <- round(df_dec[, 2:5], 2)
        names(df_dec) <- c("Region", "Pr(Adoption)", "Cond. Price (USD/tCO₂)", "Expected ECP (USD/tCO₂)", "Gated Price (USD/tCO₂)")
        cat('<div class="diffuse-only">\n\n')
        cat(sprintf("**Diffuse Hurdle Decomposition — %d:**\n\n", yr))
        print(kable(df_dec, format="html") %>%
          kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width=FALSE))
        cat("\n\n</div>\n\n")
      }
    }
  }
}

if (!has_scenario) cat('\n## Hurdle Decomposition\n\n> Scenario data not available.\n\n')
```

## Threshold Sensitivity

<div class="bulk-only">

```{r thresh-bulk, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(future_df$Bulk)) {
  p <- make_threshold_sensitivity(fit_str$Bulk, fit_adp$Bulk, future_df$Bulk,
                                   "Bulk", key_regions,
                                   str_regionFE = cfg$Bulk$regionFE, adp_regionFE = adp_cfg$regionFE)
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "thresh_sensitivity_bulk.png"), p, width=14, height=8, dpi=300)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Each panel shows projected ECP_Bulk for one key region under three adoption thresholds (0.3, 0.5, 0.7). Lower threshold → earlier onset. A wide spread between lines = projections are threshold-sensitive; a narrow spread = results are robust.
</div>

</div>

<div class="diffuse-only">

```{r thresh-diffuse, echo=FALSE, fig.width=14, fig.height=8}
if (has_scenario && !is.null(future_df$Diffuse)) {
  p <- make_threshold_sensitivity(fit_str$Diffuse, fit_adp$Diffuse, future_df$Diffuse,
                                   "Diffuse", key_regions,
                                   str_regionFE = cfg$Diffuse$regionFE, adp_regionFE = adp_cfg$regionFE)
  if (!is.null(p)) {
    print(p)
    ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, "thresh_sensitivity_diffuse.png"), p, width=14, height=8, dpi=300)
  }
} else cat("> Scenario data not available.")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this chart:** Threshold sensitivity for the Diffuse sector. Same interpretation as Bulk — a wide spread between threshold lines means results depend heavily on the threshold choice.
</div>

</div>

---

# Comparison {.tabset}

This section compares Bulk and Diffuse sector results side-by-side. Use this section to assess whether the two sectors behave similarly or diverge in their carbon pricing dynamics.

## Model Fit Comparison

```{r fit-comparison, echo=FALSE, fig.height=5}
fit_cmp <- data.frame(
  Sector    = c("Bulk", "Diffuse"),
  `R²`      = c(stats_bulk$r2,   stats_diffuse$r2),
  AIC       = c(stats_bulk$aic,  stats_diffuse$aic),
  BIC       = c(stats_bulk$bic,  stats_diffuse$bic),
  `N obs`   = c(stats_bulk$nObs, stats_diffuse$nObs),
  check.names = FALSE
)

p_r2 <- ggplot(fit_cmp, aes(x = Sector, y = `R²`,
    fill = factor(ifelse(`R²` >= 0.5, "Good", ifelse(`R²` >= 0.25, "Moderate", "Poor")),
                  levels = c("Poor", "Moderate", "Good")))) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", `R²`)), vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Poor" = col_bad, "Moderate" = col_warn, "Good" = col_good),
                    name = "Fit quality") +
  labs(title = "R² by Sector (deviance-based)", y = "R²", x = NULL) +
  ylim(0, 1) + theme_report()

p_aic <- ggplot(fit_cmp, aes(x = Sector, y = AIC, fill = Sector)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.0f", AIC)), vjust = -0.5, size = 4, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bulk" = col_bulk, "Diffuse" = col_diffuse)) +
  labs(title = "AIC by Sector (lower = better)", y = "AIC", x = NULL) +
  theme_report() + theme(legend.position = "none")

library(patchwork)
p_r2 + p_aic + plot_layout(guides = "collect") & theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read these charts:** R² (left) is the deviance-based pseudo-R² — values above 0.5 indicate a good fit (green), 0.25–0.5 moderate (orange), below 0.25 poor (red). AIC (right) measures relative model quality within each sector — lower is better, but AICs are not comparable across sectors because they are estimated on different samples. Use R² for within-sector fit quality and AIC for model selection across specifications within the same sector.
</div>

```{r fit-comparison-table, echo=FALSE}
kable(fit_cmp, format = "html", caption = "Stringency Model Fit Summary — Bulk vs Diffuse") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

## Coefficient Comparison

```{r sector-compare, echo=FALSE}
cmp <- make_comparison_table(se_bulk, se_diffuse)
if (!is.null(cmp)) {
  kable(cmp, format = "html",
        caption = "Sector Comparison — Semi-Elasticities for Shared Predictors") %>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
} else {
  cat("> Comparison table not available (one or both sector models are missing).\n")
}
```

<div class="chart-description">
**How to read this table:** Each row is a predictor that appears in both the Bulk and Diffuse model specifications. Columns show the raw log-scale coefficient (β), the semi-elasticity (% change per unit), and the p-value for each sector. Large differences in semi-elasticities between sectors indicate that the predictor has a materially different effect on industrial vs household/transport carbon pricing. Divergent significance (significant in one sector but not the other) may indicate sector-specific political economy dynamics.
</div>

## Convergence Maps {.tabset}

Difference between the hard-gated price (Section 6, threshold-based) and the expected price (Section 8, probability-weighted). **Red** = hard-gating overestimates vs the probabilistic expectation; **blue** = hard-gating underestimates. The largest discrepancies occur near the adoption threshold, where the binary gate creates a cliff that the hurdle model smooths out.

```{r conv-maps, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
conv_year <- snapshot_years[ceiling(length(snapshot_years) / 2)]
if (has_scenario && !is.null(mapping_54) && !is.null(world)) {
  for (sec in c("Bulk", "Diffuse")) {
    cat(sprintf("\n### %s (%d)\n\n", sec, conv_year))
    p <- make_convergence_map(proj_str[[sec]], hurdle[[sec]],
                               mapping_54, world, conv_year, sec)
    if (!is.null(p)) {
      print(p)
      ggsave(file.path(plotOutputDir, sprintf("conv_map_%s_%d.png", tolower(sec), conv_year)),
             p, width=12, height=6, dpi=300)
    }
    cat("\n\n")
  }
} else {
  cat("> Map rendering requires scenario data and regionmapping_54.csv.\n")
}
```

---

# Appendix {.tabset}

## Adoption Model Formulae (Gating)

```{r appendix-adp-formula, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
for (sec in c("Bulk", "Diffuse")) {
  fit <- fit_adp[[sec]]
  if (!is.null(fit)) {
    cat(sprintf("\n**%s Adoption (gating only):**\n\n", sec))
    cat("```\n")
    cat(deparse(fit$formula), "\n")
    cat("```\n\n")
  }
}
cat(sprintf("\nAdoption threshold: **%.2f**\n", adoption_threshold))
```

## Interpretation Guide

**Gaussian GLM (identity link) on log(1+ECP):** The model estimates `E[log(1+ECP)] = Xβ` (back-transform `ECP = exp(Xβ)−1`). With standardized drivers, a coefficient β is the additive effect on `log(1+ECP)` per 1-SD increase in X, i.e. it multiplies `(1+ECP)` by `exp(β)` — a multiplicative effect of `(exp(β)−1)×100`%.

**Log-transform of DV:** `ecp → log(1+ecp)` before fitting. Back-transform for display: `exp(fitted) − 1`. The +1 avoids log(0) and ensures non-negative predictions.

**Semi-elasticity:** `exp(β) − 1`. Delta-method SE: `exp(β) × SE(β)`. Testing semi = 0 is equivalent to testing β = 0, so p-values are identical to those from the coefficient table.

**Marginal Effect at Mean (MEM):** `β × E[ECP|x̄]`. Translates the log-scale coefficient into USD/tCO₂ at the sample-mean fitted price.

**Adoption gating:** Stringency models are estimated on `ecp > 0` observations only. In projections, prices are shown only where the adoption model predicts `Pr(Adoption) ≥ threshold`.

**Expected ECP:** `E[ECP] = Pr(Adoption) × Conditional_Price`. The full two-stage hurdle model output — the probabilistically-correct expected carbon price.

**Residual types:** Response residuals (observed − fitted on the `log(1+ECP)` scale) are shown throughout, appropriate for the gaussian (identity-link) model.

## Session Information

```{r session, echo=FALSE}
tryCatch(
  print(sessionInfo()),
  error = function(e) {
    cat("R Version:", R.version.string, "\n")
    cat("Platform:", R.version$platform, "\n")
  }
)
```
